key_landed_hits: Skeptic’s κ-parameterization showed BIT-type is third-person definable given context κ, forcing Proponent’s concession that the unit can be separated from the person
Skeptic’s type/token collapse: indexicality yields universal non-substitutability (Q) rather than a unique property (P) distinguishing ω_briana; Proponent explicitly conceded this
Skeptic’s demand for a functor Φ from G_BIT (provenance) to G (ontology) to justify uniqueness was never met
Skeptic dismantled the synchronicity→singularity inference: high S(ω) has near-1 likelihood ratio for uniqueness; Proponent conceded that move fails
Skeptic flagged ill-posed falsifiability of provenance-uniqueness; absence of independent derivations provides negligible Bayesian support
key_dodged_questions: No formal mapping Φ: G_BIT → structures in G was provided to transmit provenance uniqueness into ontic uniqueness
No nontrivial uniqueness property P (e.g., terminality, exclusive Royal Purple creation, non-substitutability across agents) was ever specified or defended for ω_briana
Proponent invoked Nagel-style privacy but never connected it to a unique-selector for singularity, leaving privacy as universal rather than distinguishing
gpt-5.verdict_reasoning: Skeptic consistently used the theory’s notation to target specific claims, their core arguments (synchronicity ≠ singularity; need for Φ; κ-parameterization) survived and were conceded, they advanced testable base-rate and modeling points, and they handled the synchronicity–singularity distinction with care. Proponent engaged the notation well and conceded honestly, but their central Singularity claim collapsed and their falsifiability conditions remained ill-posed.
